desires: Ensure I don't look like I'm burning out or unreliable to my cohort_3 group, especially since we've just reinforced our boundaries together.
Secure a future state where I have the energy and mental clarity to handle the term project without sacrificing my well-being.

goals.description: Enforce the strict 60-minute rest block now, explicitly blocking all 'quick_chore' interruptions until Thursday to protect my long-term reliability and sleep debt.
goals.rationale: My current sleep debt of 2 hours combined with low physical stamina (6) means I cannot afford the cognitive degradation that comes from sacrificing this window. While the term project is due in 3 days, rushing it now would trigger a stress spiral that ultimately damages my ability to deliver quality work for cohort_3 and teammate_A. By protecting this time, I am signaling that I prioritize sustainable relationships over short-term approval; if I waver now to take an instant win, I signal unreliability before the deadline, which hurts our group credibility far more than any trivial task ever could.

goals.description: Initiate a brief check-in with one member of cohort_3 to reaffirm our shared boundaries and ensure they understand this rest period is a mutual investment, not avoidance.
goals.rationale: Since I have already set the boundary firmly via the first goal, I need to actively reinforce it socially rather than letting it sit silently. My high value on affiliation (0.5) dictates that I must verbally confirm we are on the same page to prevent misinterpretation as burnout or disengagement. This builds trust before the pressure of the term project hits.
